Hey guys, welcome to the workbook for module 5. Glad you made it through the main video lesson. So just to give you a bit of a recap, the launch sequence is the moment your product first becomes available for sale. And at this point, you've shifted the beliefs, you've built anticipation and generated commitment throughout your segmentation, confirmation and pre-launch sequence. And now it's time to invite them to take that simple next step into reality. That's fully aligned with that possibility you've engineered together. So even in the case of a webinar funnel where you'll launch on the webinar itself, I still encourage you to send out a dedicated launch email later in the day as well. And this really ensures that people who either didn't sign up or signed up for the webinar but didn't show up get notified in a powerful way. And people who attended but didn't buy get a prompt and powerful follow up that also reacquaints them with the reasons they should enroll now and invest now. So what we're coaching here is going to be that progress and sustained momentum. That Rome is still burning and it's been burning forever. So extending that consistency principle into the indefinite past. And as well as a natural but urgently necessary next step. Think of that wave metaphor. You've got to ride that momentum. It's too dangerous and too hard to swim against that current. And we're going to be coaching against procrastination. Coach action by coaching against procrastination. When you launch and people assume that it's going to be around for three days, four days, five days, seven days. Procrastination is a very natural thing to occur. So we want to coach against that. One way we explore doing that is that fast action bonus. Which will help you get that initial surge of sales and act as that cart close light for non-action takers. By getting them to feel that sting of missing out a few days before the final cart close, you're in turn coaching them against inaction. You're showing them what it actually feels like. Like you are serious. You will pull this off. And doing something like this, which is natural and legitimate, especially if you're offering a fast action bonus that's not scalable and involves your time, it comes off as a lot more sincere and accepted form of using FOMO, and urgency, and scarcity than when you just pound them with those messages later on. Like five hours, four hours, three hours, one copy left of this digital version that's hosted on Teachable left. So this is a way to do it in a more authentic, natural way that is real. It doesn't just feel real, but also coaches people against procrastination. So even if they don't take you up right away, they at least know that you're serious, know that there is something they could be missing out on. And the questions I want us to explore, these are once again from the direct response 2.0, 47 questions workbook. You can explore them here again, and I suggest you do. But what is the main magic one moment or outcome your product can deliver? And why is this important to your prospect? Or why is it what they really need? And on a level deeper, why does that matter? And if you can go deeper, why does that matter? So unravel that ball of meaning, that yarn, and just kind of see where it goes. What new opportunities will arise as a result? How can we frame this outcome as a push goal that opens them up to new favorable possibilities? And what other intangible or unexpected problems does buying your product solve? And the big ones here, which we explained in the 47 questions workbook is freeing them from that search and the confidence to double down to invest their time and effort and energy wisely knowing they're going to be getting that return. And then we're going to also have those four levels of benefits. So experience the level one, which is immediate intangible, then we have our immediate emotional long-term tangible and long-term emotional. So you do want to arm yourself with a few of those before we dive in. And then we actually have some questions specific to the theme plates. The anti-launch launch theme plate, which you're soon going to see. And if you have access to six-figure emails, there's a full hour long masterclass on this. So you can go into that for a deeper dive. But if you're just rolling with what you have in front of you today, here are the questions that's going to extract the content. So what micro actions have they already taken that you can hold them consistent with? How are they all ready in the process? That's what we want to answer there. Show them they're already moving and running out of that burning building. And in what ways is Rome burning for them? How has it been burning for long before you came along? You don't want to give the perception that you started the fire. You want to give the perception that you're leading them out of it. And how will your prospect be in a worse position if they buy in a few days? What will the fire have already consumed between now and then? What benefits are they leaving on the table, even if it's just three or four days later that they buy? How have they been trying and struggling to put out the fire? And here's one that I'd love to explore. How has a product launched disingenuous to you? You know what proprietary elements have been in development testing being proven for months or years? And what results or case studies can you point to for that? So that's going to be for the anti-launch theme plate, for the Aladdin magic carpet ride theme plate, which is a similar one. And you'll see that very, very soon. Here are the additional questions. This is going to be for your minimum viable funnel where you're doing email only. So this email is going to have to work a little bit harder. But you also want to know what's the minimum viable commitment. And that's something we're going to speak a lot more to in module seven. But essentially, you know, what is the minimum barrier to get in? You know, is it tested for 30 days? Is it, you know, I know you have your doubts and hesitations, but just give it a try if it doesn't work. No harm, no foul. So what's that minimum viable commitment look like for you? And what's the quickest and most valuable when your product can provide? Also, another major topic in module seven, we're bringing it in a little bit here because this is going to be that minimum viable funnel. Your products first available. So you do want to include some more of those elements. And for each of your features and benefits, I like to add what I call a magic carpet ride moment, which is that hyper specific positive and vivid real life result that accompanies the benefit. So I put together a little table for you here so you can do this for your own product. But in the example of 10 X launches, the feature would be 20 proven deamplates. So you can, that leads to the benefit, right high converting launch emails faster and which leads to the magic carpet ride moment, fire it into convert kit while your coffee's still hot and watch your Sam cart notifications light up. So that is crazy specific, you know, one sentence here, we have convert kits, Sam cart coffee, like all these things that are super familiar to them and actually would resemble the process of, you know, achieving that benefit. So feature so you can benefit and and that should really lead you into being able to describe what it looks like. You want to show them that world. You want to engage their senses. You want to be evocative in your language. So that's what the magic carpet ride moment does. And I want you to get in the habit of doing that for all your features and benefits, because that's what adds that color to your copy. That's what makes your future pacing feel real. That's what makes the results. Your promising deal real. No one wants to make money. No one really knows what that means, but they know what it means when their Sam cart notifications light up or their PayPal lights up. You know, they know what it's and if the thing you do before that is firing an email into convert kit or drip or active campaign or infusion soft, those are things people are familiar with. So that's why we use it be hyper specific in describing that magic carpet ride moment. So that is it for the workbook. You do have a few exercises to get through before we go into the theme plates. But once you do, once you're armed with the answers to these questions, these magic carpet ride moments, the writing is going to be super simple and fast later on. So give it a go and I'll meet you back in theme plate eight, which is actually going to be a little twist on this. It's the calm before and the never too early. So I'll see you there in just a sec.
